title: Projects
subtitle: A selection of impactful projects showcasing end-to-end engineering

kyc-integration.title: KYC Integration, Mexico Carrier Compliance
kyc-integration.description: Identified and resolved a critical design gap in the KYC verification flow for US-to-Mexico shipments, preventing $2.8B in potential losses

star.situation: Amazon needed to enable a KYC plug-in requiring RFC verification for Mexican citizens receiving US → Carrier → Mexico shipments. The inherited design assumed a direct US → Carrier → MX flow
star.task: Validate the existing system design, implement the KYC integration logic, and ensure compliance for all Mexico-bound shipment routes
star.action: Conducted an independent assessment of the inherited design and discovered that Mexico used a US → Carrier1 → Amazon Carrier → MX flow, a use case the existing logic did not support. Redesigned the integration to handle the actual carrier chain, implemented the new flow, and coordinated with carrier teams (Redpack, Estafeta) to validate end-to-end
star.result: Successfully launched the KYC integration covering the correct carrier flow. Prevented $2.8B in potential losses from non-compliant shipments. Established a precedent for independent design validation on inherited systems

decisions: Performed independent design assessment rather than trusting inherited documentation
Coordinated directly with carrier partners to validate actual shipment flows
tradeoffs: Invested additional time in design validation upfront to avoid costly production issues
Chose to redesign the flow rather than patch the existing logic for long-term correctness
constraints: Inherited system design assumed a single carrier path that did not match reality
Regulatory compliance required RFC verification for all Mexico-bound shipments
scalability: New design supports additional carrier chains without code changes
Validation framework reusable for future market expansions

subscription-campaigns.title: Subscription Campaigns Deployment, LATAM Expansion
subscription-campaigns.description: Designed and executed the deployment strategy for subscription campaigns across Mexico and Brazil, preventing $7M+ in potential losses through meticulous rollback planning

star.situation: Amazon needed to extend seller subscription campaigns (similar to Prime for sellers) to Mexico and Brazil. The beta testing systems were unstable, QA processes had recently changed, and Mexico required two separate testing flows
star.task: Design a deployment strategy with comprehensive rollback planning, analyze seller creation patterns to determine optimal launch timing, and ensure campaign stability across both markets
star.action: Analyzed historical seller creation peaks and valleys to identify the safest deployment windows. Designed a phased rollout strategy with automated rollback triggers. When the testing system failed completely before delivery, proposed extending an existing campaign as a bridge solution. Maintained constant communication with stakeholders across teams
star.result: Successfully deployed subscription campaigns to both markets. Prevented $7M+ in potential losses through proactive risk analysis and rollback planning. Established deployment playbook reused for subsequent LATAM market launches

decisions: Analyzed seller creation patterns to time deployment during low-risk windows
Proposed campaign extension as bridge solution when testing infrastructure failed
tradeoffs: Accepted longer deployment timeline for reduced risk through phased rollout
Chose manual validation steps where automated testing was unavailable
constraints: Beta testing systems were unstable and failed completely before delivery
Mexico required two separate testing flows unlike other markets
scalability: Deployment playbook parameterized for future LATAM market expansions
Rollback triggers designed to be market-agnostic

aviation-forecast.title: Aviation Systems Forecast, Cruise Stability Prediction
aviation-forecast.description: Built an AI/ML model for General Electric to predict aircraft cruise stability entry points, achieving 86% balanced accuracy on 36 GB of flight data

star.situation: General Electric needed a predictive model to determine when aircraft enter stable cruise phase, using 36 GB of historical flight data across multiple aircraft types
star.task: Follow the CRISP-DM methodology end-to-end to build, evaluate, and deliver a production-ready prediction model with the highest possible balanced accuracy
star.action: Executed the full CRISP-DM pipeline: data understanding, preparation, modeling, evaluation, and deployment. Implemented and compared multiple model architectures including XGBoost, Neural Networks (LSTM and Dense), Logistic Regression, and LightGBM. Performed extensive feature engineering and hyperparameter tuning
star.result: Achieved 86% balanced accuracy (best: 86.77%) with the Neural Network model, significantly outperforming baseline approaches. Delivered a complete model pipeline following CRISP-DM methodology

decisions: Compared 5 model architectures to find the best fit for the data characteristics
Selected Neural Network (Dense + LSTM) as final model based on balanced accuracy metric
tradeoffs: Chose balanced accuracy over raw accuracy to handle class imbalance in flight phases
Invested in multiple model comparisons rather than optimizing a single architecture
constraints: Private client data required secure handling and could not leave GE infrastructure
36 GB dataset required efficient data pipeline design for iterative experimentation
scalability: Model pipeline designed for retraining with new flight data batches
Feature engineering pipeline reusable across different aircraft types
